name: ktpanda-modules

metadata.name: ktpanda-modules
metadata.summary: A collection of miscellaneous utility modules

documentation:
ktpanda_modules
===============

A collection of helper modules by PinkPandaKatie (https://ktpanda.org)


threadpool
----------

Maintains a pool of threads which can execute jobs.


object_pool
-----------

Maintains a pool of objects which can be checked out as needed by different threads.


hotload
-------

A class which can reload its methods from a module when it detects changes.


sqlite_helper
-------------

A wrapper class for an SQLite database that includes schema versioning and
various helper methods.

textcolor
---------

A module for coloring text using VT100 codes


ttyutil
-------

Utilities for controlling Posix TTY devices and reading raw input.


dateutils
---------

Simple utilities for dealing with date / time, extending the built-in datetime.


fileutils
---------

Simple utilities for dealing with files / directories.

roundtrip_encoding
------------------

Contains functions for converting between text and binary data in a way that unmodified
text data will be converted to the same binary data.

vt100
-----

Defines constants for VT100 control codes.


cli
---

Defines decorators which make it easy to create dispatch-style CLI interfaces.

xmledit
-------

Parses an XML document using Expat, and allows replacing nodes and text without affecting
the surrounding formatting.

modules.name: ktpanda.roundtrip_encoding

roundtrip_encoding

TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM, OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE
SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE SOFTWARE.

functions.name: ktpanda.roundtrip_encoding.roundtrip_decode
documentation:
Convert a binary file into text in a way that can be converted back with the same
    encoding.

    If the file begins with the bytes FF FE, then it is decoded as UTF-16LE. If it starts
    with FE FF, it is decoded as UTF-16BE. Otherwise, the file is assumed to be UTF-8.

    `fallback_encoding`, if set, should be a single-byte encoding that maps every possible
    byte to a unique Unicode codepoint (such as 'iso-8859-1'), otherwise round-trip
    encoding cannot be guaranteed.

    If decoding fails, and `fallback_encoding` is not set, then the file will be decoded
    as UTF-8 with the 'surrogateescape' error handler

    `encoding` and `errors` can be set to force a specific encoding, but round-trip
    encoding cannot be guaranteed in that case.

    If `remove_bom` is True (default), then if the file starts with a byte-order mark
    (U+FEFF), it will be removed from the resulting text and noted in the returned
    RoundTripEncoding.

    If `convert_line_endings` is True (default), and the file contains *only* DOS line
    endings ('
'), then all line endings will be converted to '
' and noted in the
    returned encoding. If `convert_line_endings` is False, `line_endings` will be set to
    'raw'.

    The following is guaranteed to be true for all byte streams when `encoding`, `errors`,
    and `fallback_encoding` are left at default values:

    >>> text, encoding = roundtrip_decode(input_data)
    >>> assert input_data == roundtrip_encode(text, encoding)

functions.name: ktpanda.roundtrip_encoding.roundtrip_encode
documentation:
Encode `text` using the parameters of `encoding`. First, if `encoding.line_endings`
    is 'dos', then all instances of '
' are replaced with '
', Then, if
    `encoding.has_bom` is `True`, then a byte-order mark is prepended to the text.
    Finally, the text is encoded using `encoding.encoding` and `encoding.errors` and the
    resulting `bytes` object is returned.

classes.name: ktpanda.roundtrip_encoding.TextContent

methods.name: ktpanda.roundtrip_encoding.TextContent.get_text
documentation: Retrieves the current text, converting it from binary if necessary

methods.name: ktpanda.roundtrip_encoding.TextContent.get_binary
documentation: Retrieves the current binary data, converting it from text if necessary.

methods.name: ktpanda.roundtrip_encoding.TextContent.set_text
documentation: Set the text content and clear cached binary content.

methods.name: ktpanda.roundtrip_encoding.TextContent.set_binary
documentation: Set the binary content and clear cached text content.
Manages the contents of a file, converting between text and binary as necessary.

functions.name: ktpanda.textutil.roundtrip_decode
documentation:
Convert a binary file into text in a way that can be converted back with the same
    encoding.

    If the file begins with the bytes FF FE, then it is decoded as UTF-16LE. If it starts
    with FE FF, it is decoded as UTF-16BE. Otherwise, the file is assumed to be UTF-8.

    `fallback_encoding`, if set, should be a single-byte encoding that maps every possible
    byte to a unique Unicode codepoint (such as 'iso-8859-1'), otherwise round-trip
    encoding cannot be guaranteed.

    If decoding fails, and `fallback_encoding` is not set, then the file will be decoded
    as UTF-8 with the 'surrogateescape' error handler

    `encoding` and `errors` can be set to force a specific encoding, but round-trip
    encoding cannot be guaranteed in that case.

    If `remove_bom` is True (default), then if the file starts with a byte-order mark
    (U+FEFF), it will be removed from the resulting text and noted in the returned
    RoundTripEncoding.

    If `convert_line_endings` is True (default), and the file contains *only* DOS line
    endings ('
'), then all line endings will be converted to '
' and noted in the
    returned encoding. If `convert_line_endings` is False, `line_endings` will be set to
    'raw'.

    The following is guaranteed to be true for all byte streams when `encoding`, `errors`,
    and `fallback_encoding` are left at default values:

    >>> text, encoding = roundtrip_decode(input_data)
    >>> assert input_data == roundtrip_encode(text, encoding)

functions.name: ktpanda.textutil.roundtrip_encode
documentation:
Encode `text` using the parameters of `encoding`. First, if `encoding.line_endings`
    is 'dos', then all instances of '
' are replaced with '
', Then, if
    `encoding.has_bom` is `True`, then a byte-order mark is prepended to the text.
    Finally, the text is encoded using `encoding.encoding` and `encoding.errors` and the
    resulting `bytes` object is returned.

functions.name: ktpanda.dateutils.timestamp_to_unix
documentation: Given a text timestamp with optional timezone offset, convert it to a UNIX timestamp.

functions.name: ktpanda.dateutils.localtime_to_unix
documentation: Attempt to guess what the correct universal time is for the given local time.

functions.name: ktpanda.dateutils.isotime
documentation: Convert a unix time to a ISO-8601 timestamp in GMT.

functions.name: ktpanda.dateutils.parse_datespec
documentation:
Given a "date specification", return the date identified. `txt` can either be a
full ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD), a partial (MM-DD), or a single number, which is
interpreted as "days before today", where 0 is today, 1 is yesterday, etc. Can also be
"t" for today or "y" for yesterday.

functions.name: ktpanda.dateutils.parse_duration
documentation:
Parse a duration into seconds. Duration can either be H:M:S, or something like
'3h', '2h15m', etc.

functions.name: ktpanda.dateutils.split_time
documentation:
Split `secs` into hours, minutes, seconds, and fractions. It should always be the
case that `sum(split_time(x)) == x`, barring rounding errors.

functions.name: ktpanda.dateutils.hms
documentation: Convert `secs` into HH:MM:SS.

sqlite_helper
=============

A wrapper class for an SQLite database that includes schema versioning and

functions.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.in_transaction
documentation:
Decorator for methods which write to the database which wraps the function in a
call to `retry_transaction`. `mode` is "DEFERRED" (default), "IMMEDIATE", or
"EXCLUSIVE". If specified `attr` is the name of an attribute on the current object
which references the SQLiteDB object (to support the delegate model).

functions.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.split_schema
documentation:
Split `text` into a list of individual SQL statements. Each statement should be
terminated by a double-semicolon (;;). This allows for the definition of triggers
which contain multiple statements.

classes.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.Cursor
documentation: Subclass of sqlite3.Cursor which supports use as a context manager.

methods.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.SQLiteDB.exec_schema
documentation: Executes an array of statements directly

methods.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.SQLiteDB.execute_nonquery
documentation:
Execute a statement that will update rows but not return them, like 'INSERT',
'UPDATE', or 'DELETE'. Returns a tuple of (rowcount, last_insert_rowid).

methods.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.SQLiteDB.query_list
documentation: Runs the given query in full and returns rows as a list.

methods.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.SQLiteDB.query_one
documentation: Returns a single row from the query. If the query returns no rows, returns `default`.

methods.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.SQLiteDB.query_scalar
documentation: Returns a single column from a single row. If the query returns no rows, returns `default`.

methods.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.SQLiteDB.retry_transaction
documentation:
Begin a transaction and run func(). If the database is locked, rolls back and
runs func() again until it succeeds. If it fails with any other exception, the
database is rolled back

classes.name: ktpanda.sqlite_helper.Query
documentation: Utility class for building an SQL query

functions.name: ktpanda.fileutils._save_file
documentation: Save a file to path, creating a temporary file while writing, then atomically replacing

functions.name: ktpanda.fileutils.save_file
documentation:
Writes `data` to a path, first writing to a temporary file, then atomically
replacing the destination. If `data` is a `bytes` object, the file is opened in binary
mode. If `data` is a `str`, the file is opened as text, with the default encoding of
`"utf8"`. If `data` is None, then a context manager is returned allowing the file to
be incrementally written. Extra keyword arguments are passed to
`tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile`.

functions.name: ktpanda.fileutils.save_json
documentation: Write `data` (a JSON object) to `path` using `save_file`.

functions.name: ktpanda.fileutils.load_json
documentation: Load JSON from the given path, returning `default` if the file does not exist.

modules.name: ktpanda.git_helper
documentation:
git_helper
==========

Contains a class, `Git`, for working with a Git repository

functions.name: ktpanda.git_helper._quote_arg
documentation:
Quote an argument for debug output. Not intended as a general-purpose shell
quoting function.

methods.name: ktpanda.git_helper.Git.add_token
documentation: Adds `self.token` as a password to the given URL if a username is present.

methods.name: ktpanda.git_helper.Git.temp_remote
documentation:
Context manager to temporarily add a remote to the configuration file. Note that
any repository configuration changes made within the block may be reverted
afterward.

If `url' is a plain name, then it is returned verbatim and nothing is added to
the config file.

methods.name: ktpanda.git_helper.Git.run
documentation: Run a Git command, printing the output to STDOUT

methods.name: ktpanda.git_helper.Git.get_toplevel
documentation: Updates repo_path to be the top level path for the repository.

methods.name: ktpanda.git_helper.Git.parse_status_v1
documentation: Parses the output from `git status --porcelain=v1 -z`

methods.name: ktpanda.git_helper.Git.push_remote_branch
documentation:
Push a commit directly to a remote branch.

`remote` can be a named remote (e.g. 'origin') or a direct URL.

`branch` is the name of the remote branch to create/update (doesn't even have to
be a local branch).

`commit` is a commit hash, local branch, or tag. If not specified, the current
commit is selected.

If `force` is True, then the branch will be updated even if it is not a
fast-forward.

methods.name: ktpanda.git_helper.Git.set_remote_tag
documentation: Set a tag on a remote repository

methods.name: ktpanda.git_helper.Git.delete_remote_tag
documentation: Delete a tag from a remote repository
